body:
I am going to put a stop to these terrible economic policies the second I become president. |

Friend, Where are all the jobs? The Obama Administration released their updated jobs report today, and once again, we see more proof that Obama-Clinton economic policies are killing the American middle class. I’ve been a job-creator my whole life. While DC politicians have been failing, I’ve been building, investing and hiring. And I’m going to push a strong pro-jobs agenda the second I become your president. Please help me get there with one urgent donation. CONTRIBUTE $35 CONTRIBUTE $75 CONTRIBUTE $100 CONTRIBUTE OTHER AMOUNT The White House and the Clinton campaign will spin the numbers, pat themselves on the back for all the “success” they’ve had on the economy, but Americans like you aren’t buying it anymore. It’s been the same story for eight years: food stamps, welfare, and unemployment are too high, while job growth, home ownership, and business startups are way too low. Enough, Friend. The people have had enough. And thanks to loyal supporters like you, I am going to put a stop to these terrible economic policies the second I become president. The DC elites have rigged the economy against you. That ENDS next January as we: *** Secure the border and deport illegal aliens; *** Undo disastrous trade deals that have sent jobs to China and Mexico; and, *** Cut taxes and regulations to keep jobs in the USA. I will fight for you and other middle class Americans. I know how the economy works and I can get our economy moving so we can grow jobs and help families get ahead. So let’s put an end to these terrible jobs reports. Let’s put American workers first. Let’s win – make one donation of $35, $75 or $100. Thank you! Donald Trump

there are only 32 days left and we need your help. |

Friend, we are in the critical, final days of the election, and Donald Trump and our House and Senate candidates need your help. A record number of Republicans showed up to vote in the primaries and we have a chance to turn those voters out and elect Donald Trump President of the United States, preserve our Senate Majority and elect more Republicans to the House. I know you understand how important this opportunity is for our country and that is why we need your help to win. With early voting already underway, we must do everything in our power NOW to reach voters and get GOP ads on the airwaves. Friend, I would like to humbly ask you today for a special Get-Out-The-Vote gift of $35, $50, $100, $250 or even just $5. Without a strong push to carry our message of strength, prosperity and freedom we could lose on Election Day. Mr. Trump and all our candidates nationwide count on your vote and financial help to win. I want them all to know that the grassroots of our party are standing with them in this battle to Make America Great Again. Please join me now with a contribution of $35, $50, $100, $250 or even just $5 and let’s take our country back. There is so much on the line over these last weeks, I hope you’re with us. Your friend, Newt Gingrich Former Speaker of the House CONTRIBUTE $100

name: Pat Murray (via VanHollen.org)
subject: tune in:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
Friend, I first met Chris discussing our different musical tastes, favorite movies and yes -- debating nuclear weapons policy and the nuclear freeze movement. Little did I know that 30 years later his passion to make our communities and our world a safer and better place would still be a defining theme of our lives together.

But I should have guessed, because Chris has always been a strong believer that government can be an important force for good. Sadly, we are living in a time when the radical ideology of groups like the Tea Party are undermining faith in our democracy. For the last 6 years Republican control of the Congress has enabled these extremists to control the agenda and block efforts to make progress.

That's why Washington Republicans and their big-money friends are determined not to lose their majority. They're trying to turn the tide of this election in races across the country--and Maryland won't be an exception.

Over the past few months, top Republicans like Scott Walker and Carly Fiorina have been helping fund our Republican opponent's agenda. They -- just like the NRA and a shady SuperPAC that has already started attacking Chris -- know that our opponent will stand with them if she's elected.

Chris won't.

Chris has spent his time in public service fighting to keep dark money out of politics and end gun violence, and advancing the issues that matter most to the people of Maryland. And when he's elected to the U.S. Senate, his opponent's big-money friends know that he'll keep fighting for our values and priorities.

Early voting begins in Maryland in less than 30 days, friend. Will you chip in $ or whatever you can right now to help keep us reach our September goal before Friday's FEC deadline?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Donate $5 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $10 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $25 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $50 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $100 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $200 to stand with Chris >>>

Or donate another amount to stand with Chris >>>

Polls show that 19% of the electorate in Maryland is still undecided. We need to do better than that. With less than one month until early voting we must do everything we can to turn out as many voters as we possible and move our state and our country forward.

Thank you for being part of this,

Katherine
Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
You're receiving this email at: dpwillis67@gmail.com
If you'd like to unsubscribe visit: http://action.vanhollen.org/unsubscribe

name: Ben Ray
subject: Just say no (to Koch)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, 133 W
body:
| [ ](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a5051d/7be36b49/1e46498f/78f8327e/1357840742/VEsH/ "http://www.evanbayhforindiana.com/")
---
Friend -- The _New York Times_ just reported that "the vast and cash-rich Koch political network is intensifying efforts behind [their] preferred... candidates."

And in Indiana, their preferred candidate is Congressman Todd Young. The Kochs are all over our state, contacting voters and "urging them to reject Evan Bayh."

This is exactly what's wrong with our political system! **The Kochs and their Super PACs are trying to _buy Indiana 's Senate seat._** If we don't fight back TODAY, they could wipe out Evan's lead, and we'll be stuck with their puppet as a senator.

**Just say no to Koch, friend.[Pitch in $5 to fight the Kochs' millions with Hoosier grassroots power and win this election with our superior ground game.](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a5051d/7be36b49/1e46498f/78f8327d/1357840742/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bayh-email?refcode=20161007nd&amount=10")**

No wonder the Kochs love Congressman Young:
* They recently gave him a 93% approval rating
* He agrees with them that workers whose jobs are outsourced don't deserve our help
* He supports their plans to slash Social Security and Medicare

**But together, we can stop them. Help Indiana say no to Koch:[Chip in $5 to beat the Super PACs and win this race on the ground.](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a5051d/7be36b49/1e46498f/78f8327d/1357840742/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bayh-email?refcode=20161007nd&amount=10")**

name: Kent Gates
subject: Justice Proves Why Not West Virginia
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF COLE
body:
| |
---
| **_Jim Justice has proven by his actions you can’t trust him._**

**CHARLESTON, WV** – When Jim Justice announced his campaign for Governor, he asked the crowd to join in him in reciting the phrase, “Why Not West Virginia?” He [said](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsoundcloud.com%2Fkent-gates%2Fjim-justice-trust-me&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=pr_1007&n=1&v=1) on Talkline with Hoppy Kercheval that voters should simply “Trust Me!” He challenged people at the West Virginia Chamber of Commerce Business Summit to “name one thing that Jim Justice said he would do that he hasn’t.” **The Answers:** Why Not West Virginia? You have proven you can’t be trusted. Trust Me? You have proven you can’t be trusted. Name One Thing? Pay your taxes, fines, and bills. Today _NPR_ [broke](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwvpublic.org%2Fpost%2Fbillionaire-gubernatorial-candidate-owes-15-million-taxes-and-fines&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=pr_1007&n=2&v=1) a blockbuster story detailing the some $15 million in unpaid fines and taxes that Jim Justice and his companies have left littered across six states. Fines for mine safety violations that put workers at risk. In fact, he has more than any other coal operator in the country. Unpaid taxes that hurt county and local governments and leave schools with the lack of funds to teach children. What was Jim Justice’s response? He refused repeated attempts by _NPR_ to talk about his troubles. His campaign spokesman is quoted in the story saying Jim Justice “won't be doing an interview," “While Jim Justice has $2.6 million lying around in his couch cushions to fund his campaign for Governor, he seems to have no money to pay his $15 million in unpaid taxes and fines. Yet, he asks voters to simply ‘trust him’ to be their Governor,” asserted Cole for Governor Spokesman Kent Gates. “Actions speak louder than words. And, Jim Justice has proven by placing his political campaign ahead of his obligations that he can’t be trusted. If voters can’t trust him to pay his taxes, bills and fines, how could they ever trust him to be their Governor?” **Justice failed to pay $1.4 million in West Virginia taxes.** In 2014, one of his companies accounted for $1.4 million in unpaid coal severance taxes – one-third of the amount owed. One of the biggest factors that contributed to West Virginia’s crippling budget deficit was a severe reduction in severance tax collections, which Jim Justice thinks he doesn’t have to pay. “West Virginia is having trouble collecting taxes. Jim Justice if you would do your part it would help,’ said Gates. “It would help fix our roads, provide more funding for teachers and fight the drug epidemic crippling West Virginia.” **Highlights from the story:** _But an NPR investigation shows that Justice's mining companies still fail to pay millions of dollars in mine safety penalties two years after_[ _an earlier investigation documented_](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.npr.org%2F2014%2F11%2F15%2F363024382%2Fbillionaire-spent-millions-in-charity-but-avoided-mine-fines&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=pr_1007&n=3&v=1) _the same behavior. Our analysis of federal data shows that Justice is now the nation's top mine safety delinquent._ _His mining companies owe $15 million in six states, including property and minerals taxes, state coal severance and withholding taxes, and federal income, excise and unemployment taxes, as well as mine safety penalties, according to county, state and federal records._ _In the past 16 months, while fines and taxes went unpaid, Justice personally contributed nearly $2.9 million in interest-free loans and in-kind contributions to his gubernatorial campaign, according to state campaign finance reports._ _NPR conducted another analysis of federal mine safety data exactly two years later and found that mines owned by Justice added $1.38 million in new and unpaid mine safety penalties._ _The MSHA data also show that those mines were cited for 3,657 violations while they were delinquent, including 699 violations that are classified by MSHA as factors in mine fatalities, fatal mine accidents and major disasters._ _**"Kentucky Fuel's history regarding the payment of penalties is abysmal," the commission wrote. "The operator's record indicates that it has repeatedly disregarded final penalty assessments."**_ _In Knott County, Ky., multiple lawsuits were filed against Justice's Kentucky Fuel Corp. A suit last year sought $2.7 million for three years of back taxes. That triggered about $300,000 in payments, but those suddenly stopped in April, says Knott County Judge-Executive Zachary Weinberg. Weinberg calls Kentucky Fuel a "habitual offender" that is denying the county critical funds._ _NPR found in county records in South Carolina, Tennessee, Kentucky, Virginia and West Virginia more than $6 million in state and federal tax liens for failure to pay state withholding and coal severance taxes, and federal income, excise and unemployment taxes._ _**Most of the state and federal liens — more than $4.5 million worth — are in Justice's home state of West Virginia, and more than 60 percent of that debt is for state coal severance taxes.**_ _**Weinberg believes Justice has the resources to pay what his companies owe.**_[_**Forbes**_](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.forbes.com%2Fprofile%2Fjim-justice-ii%2F&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=pr_1007&n=4&v=1) _**says Justice is the richest person in West Virginia and worth $1.56 billion. That's plenty for Weinberg. "He could pay it one way or the other," he says.**_ ##

name: Bill Bryant
subject: ICYMI: Our choice for Washington’s governor
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Washingtonians for Bryant
body:
[Update Profile / Unsubscribe](http://emarketing.voter-science.com/private/igrfw8d7se/2I6/FP87cHS/unsubscribe/27634621)

---
| |
---
| | ****
---
| **
Our choice for Washington’s governor**
If dollars were votes, Democrat Jay Inslee would easily win a second term as Washington’s governor. He has raised more than $8 million for his re-election campaign.
By comparison, Republican challenger Bill Bryant has raised only about $3 million, stacking the political odds against him.

But this election isn’t about money. It’s about leadership, and the willingness to work for both sides of the state. And it’s about pragmaticism, not pie-in-the-sky schemes to save the planet on the backs of Washington farmers, businesses and consumers.

That’s why we support Bryant.

Inslee is a westside politician. His supporters are there, and so is their money. A review of his state campaign contribution report shows the vast majority of his 29,725 contributions came from the westside. We counted a little more than two dozen contributions from people who identified themselves as either farmers or ranchers — and one of them had a Seattle address.

To put that in perspective, 1,153 of Inslee’s contributions came from people identifying themselves as attorneys.

Some 7,027 of Inslee’s contributions came from Seattle, which has a population of 608,660, versus 785 that came from Spokane, the second largest city in the state with a population of 208,916.

That tells us what we could expect in a second Inslee term — more westside ideas such as the carbon cap, which he has ordered the state Department of Ecology to cook up without including the legislature, and more regulations on dairies and other facets of agriculture.

On the other hand, Bryant knows and understands farming, business and trade. He was a Seattle port commissioner and has owned a trade consultancy for two decades. He is also a familiar face on the eastside of Washington. More than 250 contributions to his campaign have come from farmers, ranchers, fruit growers and processors.

They trust Bryant to work for them and other interests around the state that count on a square deal.

A square deal is not what the Washington wheat industry got from Inslee in 2014. The governor sided with the International Longshore Workers Union in its dispute with the United Grain Co. terminal in Longview. His employees, state Department of Agriculture grain inspectors, were harassed and threatened by ILWU members. Instead of continuing to protect the grain inspectors and arresting and prosecuting the perpetrators, Inslee withdrew state patrol protection. That forced the company either to cease operations or bow to the union.

That in turn put much of the $1 billion Washington wheat industry at risk. Upwards of 90 percent of the wheat grown in the state is exported, and none of it can be shipped until it is inspected.

Inslee’s favoritism toward the ILWU also emboldened the union later that year to embark on a five-month work slowdown at container ports that cost Washington farmers and processors $770 million, according to a study by the Washington Council on International Trade.

Washington agriculture can’t afford Inslee’s brand of “leadership.”

It’s time for a change in Washington’s Capitol. It’s time for someone who knows and understands agriculture, trade and business.

That candidate is Bill Bryant.

_You can read the editorial at the Capital Press[HERE](http://emarketing.voter-science.com/c/27634621/489/FP87cHS/igrfw8d7se?redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.capitalpress.com%2FOpinion%2FEditorials%2F20161006%2Four-choice-for-washingtons-governor)_

He is also a familiar face on the eastside of Washington. More than 250 contributions to his campaign have come from farmers, ranchers, fruit growers and processors. They trust Bryant to work for them and other interests around the state that count on a square deal. A square deal is not what the Washington wheat industry got from Inslee in 2014. The governor sided with the International Longshore Workers Union in its dispute with the United Grain Co. terminal in Longview. His employees, state Department of Agriculture grain inspectors, were harassed and threatened by ILWU members. Instead of continuing to protect the grain inspectors and arresting and prosecuting the perpetrators, Inslee withdrew state patrol protection. That forced the company either to cease operations or bow to the union. That in turn put much of the $1 billion Washington wheat industry at risk. Upwards of 90 percent of the wheat grown in the state is exported, and none of it can be shipped until it is inspected. Inslee’s favoritism toward the ILWU also emboldened the union later that year to embark on a five-month work slowdown at container ports that cost Washington farmers and processors $770 million, according to a study by the Washington Council on International Trade. Washington agriculture can’t afford Inslee’s brand of “leadership.” It’s time for a change in Washington’s Capitol. It’s time for someone who knows and understands agriculture, trade and business. That candidate is Bill Bryant. You can read the editorial at the Capital Press | | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Washingtonians for Bryant PO Box 2121, Tacoma, WA 98401

body:
You can’t vote for Donald Trump if you aren’t registered. Help the Trump campaign conduct a last-minute nationwide voter-registration push for pro-Trump Americans. |

Friend, Your name was forwarded to me as an active Republican who knows a lot about politics and campaigns. So I’m betting you are all set to vote on November 8th. But there are thousands of pro-Trump Americans in key states who are not yet registered, and we need their votes in order to win. That’s why I am asking you to please make one urgent donation to to fund our last-minute voter registration efforts. DONATE TO THE TRUMP REGISTRATION DRIVE My father has inspired a nationwide groundswell of patriotic support. But many of these pro-Trump Americans have been left behind so badly by the politicians, that they are not registered to vote at their current addresses. We can’t let these Americans be left behind again. Not with so much at stake on November 8th. We’re working on the ground with grassroots teams in key areas to get as many of these citizens registered as we possibly can. Your donation right now will help us make sure these Trump supporters will be able to vote on Election Day. Hillary Clinton has union bosses and a small army of paid liberal campaign workers registering voters door-to-door in Democrat neighborhoods. Don’t let them win. My father always has the best team around him, and we’ve got a great team in the field right now signing up pro-Trump Americans to vote, but we need your financial help ASAP to get the job done. Thank you! Donald J. Trump Jr. DONATE TO THE TRUMP VOTER REGISTRATION DRIVE

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: after today's debate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
Friend, I first met Chris discussing our different musical tastes, favorite movies and yes -- debating nuclear weapons policy and the nuclear freeze movement. Little did I know that 30 years later his passion to make our communities and our world a safer and better place would still be a defining theme of our lives together.

But I should have guessed, because Chris has always been a strong believer that government can be an important force for good. Sadly, we are living in a time when the radical ideology of groups like the Tea Party are undermining faith in our democracy. For the last 6 years Republican control of the Congress has enabled these extremists to control the agenda and block efforts to make progress.

That's why Washington Republicans and their big-money friends are determined not to lose their majority. They're trying to turn the tide of this election in races across the country--and Maryland won't be an exception.

Over the past few months, top Republicans like Scott Walker and Carly Fiorina have been helping fund our Republican opponent's agenda. They -- just like the NRA and a shady SuperPAC that has already started attacking Chris -- know that our opponent will stand with them if she's elected.

Chris won't.

Chris has spent his time in public service fighting to keep dark money out of politics and end gun violence, and advancing the issues that matter most to the people of Maryland. And when he's elected to the U.S. Senate, his opponent's big-money friends know that he'll keep fighting for our values and priorities.

Early voting begins in Maryland in less than 30 days, friend. Will you chip in $ or whatever you can right now to help keep us reach our September goal before Friday's FEC deadline?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Donate $5 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $10 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $25 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $50 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $100 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $200 to stand with Chris >>>

Or donate another amount to stand with Chris >>>

Polls show that 19% of the electorate in Maryland is still undecided. We need to do better than that. With less than one month until early voting we must do everything we can to turn out as many voters as we possible and move our state and our country forward.

Thank you for being part of this,

Katherine
Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate

name: Aisling, jayinslee.com
subject: Bryant’s ad - response needed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Jay's opponent has launched his October Surprise.

Dear Friend,

Jay's opponent has just gone on the air with an attack ad -- we need your help to respond.

This is just the opening salvo in what will be a barrage of false, negative attacks. Republican Bill Bryant is making a last desperate attempt to win this election by flooding the airwaves with untrue ads, and we can't let him get away with it.

With help from a few rich friends with deep pockets, Bryant will now throw everything he can into this race. He's hoping something -- anything -- will stick.

We must respond. Will you rush a donation to help us fight back immediately against Bryant's negative ad blitz?

http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTY1MjgyMgS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=MTAyMDU1NzE1OAS2&mt=1&rt=0

Look, while Governor Inslee offers real solutions for our schools, our economy, and our environment, Bill Bryant hides behind deceptive, false attacks.

Now it's up to all of us to stand with Jay, because we cannot afford to be outspent as ballots are getting ready to drop. Bill Bryant and the Republican machine will spend and spend with help from their wealthy backers -- it's up to our grassroots army to respond.

Step up now with a donation of $5 to Jay's campaign. Please don't delay -- it's urgent.

http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTY1MjgyMgS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=MTAyMDU1NzE1OAS2&mt=1&rt=0

Thanks for standing with Jay.

Aisling

| Hi everyone -- I hope you're staying safe in this storm and its aftermath. I'd like to remember those who lost their lives to this dangerous storm, both in the U.S. and abroad. I'm grateful to our first responders and the National Guard for serving and protecting our communities even in dangerous conditions.

I wanted to reach out with some guidelines for dealing with the aftermath of Hurricane Matthew as it continues to affect our state. First of all, if you need assistance, you can call the Florida Emergency Information Line at 800-342-3557 or 911 for immediate emergencies.

Here's some steps for hurricane cleanup to keep you and your loved ones safe:
* Check on your neighbors. Clear debris on and around your property.
* Stay clear of downed power lines and report them to Florida Power and Light at 1-(800)-4-OUTAGE (1-800-468-8243).
* Limit travel until power is restored and roads are clear. If you come across flooded roadways, turn around and take an alternative route.
* Empty standing water from your property and wear bug repellent as mosquitoes that may carry the Zika virus breed in standing water.

Lastly, if you are in a position to help, [**sign up to volunteer**](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2d8a/738133e4/3131ecff/7f857079/626783268/VEsH/ "http://www.redcross.org/volunteer/become-a-volunteer#step1")or [**donate to the Red Cross.**](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2d8a/738133e4/3131ecff/7f85707a/626783268/VEsE/ "https://www.redcross.org/donate/donation")

Sincerely,
Patrick Murphy

|

---

| Greetings,
---
| We’re down to the final month of the general election, which means Team Scott is working extra hard to earn the vote of every Vermonter who wants to see the economy grow and Vermont become more affordable – which will be Phil’s top priorities as Governor. Check out this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign.

**EARLY VOTING IN PROGRESS – HAVE YOU VOTED FOR PHIL YET?**
Early voting is now open. You’ve kept us going throughout the campaign, now it’s time to pull the lever for Phil. **Don’t wait until November 8.** Voting early ensures nothing keeps you from the voting booth on election day, and helps us maximize our Get Out The Vote efforts for the remainder of the campaign. Click [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=650a25b6f6&e=19cebfe173) to find your town clerk's location or to request an absentee ballot.

And, ICYMI, we’ve released our first TV ad of the General Election this week. The choice is clear! Watch it [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=119c163105&e=19cebfe173), share it with friends, and then get to your town clerk’s office to vote early!
***
**PHIL’S ECONOMIC PLANS: WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT**
Last month Phil released a comprehensive economic development plan (which you can view [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=25064d410c&e=19cebfe173)) that laid out his blueprint for economic growth. While his opponent is trying to jump on the economic messaging bandwagon in the final weeks of the campaign, **Phil remains the only candidate with a comprehensive approach to strengthening the economy and making Vermont more affordable**. This week, we want to share more about Phil’s proposals to grow our workforce and address our demographic challenges:
Vermont faces significant demographic challenges as our population gets older, workers retire, and younger generations leave the state. A smaller workforce means a higher tax burden on those who remain. As Governor, **Phil will support a number of actions to retain and attract more working-age Vermonters, grow our workforce and ease the tax burden**. Those actions include **supporting _innovation in the public education system_ to make Vermont more appealing for young families; setting goals and working with Vermont colleges and universities _to retain more college graduates_ ; _investing in skilled workers_ and _creating training programs_ to increase the number of skilled workers; and eliminating the “benefits cliff” to increase independence and _bring more Vermonters into the workforce_**. Read more [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=64049ed00b&e=19cebfe173).

Photo Credit: @PhilScottVT

***
**VERMONTERS DESERVE MORE FROM SHUMLIN ON HIS ALL-PAYER PLANS**
In his final months in office, Gov. Shumlin is working to push through a transition to an all-payer model, which would significantly impact our healthcare system, asking doctors to sign up as part of one giant Accountable Care Organization.

This is a complicated transition, and one where the outcome is unknown. This week, Phil called on Gov. Shumlin and the Green Mountain Cardboard to add additional public presentations and more transparency on the model. “Vermonters deserve to have a detailed discussion about this before we move forward. **We simply cannot afford any more expensive and unsuccessful healthcare experiments**. I think it’s premature for the Shumlin Administration to lock Vermonters into an agreement, without first explaining how, exactly, it is going to work and what, if any, savings Vermonters can expect to see in the cost of care and in their insurance premiums,” Phil said in a statement.

An editorial in the [_St. Albans Messenger_](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f9320dca42&e=19cebfe173) rightly points out that, yet again, Vermont will be charging forward on an experiment without knowing the impact on consumers and businesses. We saw how that worked with Vermont Health Connect! As the _Messenger_ writes, “When the agreement is signed, Vermont will be the first in the nation to test the idea. **No one knows whether it will be a good thing, or a disaster**. But it would seem axiomatic that **when we decide to turn upside down an industry that constitutes 20 percent of our economy that we do a more thorough job of analyzing the pros and cons**. Publicly.” We agree. Read Phil’s full statement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f68c75eb36&e=19cebfe173).
*******
**INCREASING COSTS ON TRANSPORTATION WRONG FOR VERMONT**
As Phil said this week, his opponent’s so-called “cap and trade” plan for transportation would add unnecessary costs onto Vermont drivers and businesses, slow the economy and deepen the crisis of affordability facing many families and employers. In his response, Phil reinforced his commitment to growing the economy and making Vermont affordable “so we can afford to invest in priorities like early education and job training.”

“I’m the only candidate in this race who knows that **we need to be working to reduce the tax burden. To do that, we have to keep the state budget from growing faster than the economy and wages; stop imposing new taxes and fees; modernize state government; and make growing the economy the highest priority.** If we don’t come to grips with this reality, we’ll continue to face higher taxes, a shrinking labor force and fewer resources to invest in programs we all support like early education, job training and substance abuse prevention and recovery,” Phil said. Read the full statement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=81d4a9cbe8&e=19cebfe173).

Photo Credit: @Thunder_Truck

*******
**Forums and Friends**
Another exciting week of debates, events and hearing from hundreds of Vermonters about what matters to them – the economy! This week Phil and Team Scott joined Republicans from around the state at the **Vermont Republican Party Fall Dinner** , and stopped by **Jet Service Envelope Company** and the **Vermont Pie & Pasta Company** to check out some great small businesses. Phil also participated in the **Vermont PBS debate** and forums with **American Academy of Pediatrics Vermont Chapter** , **Permanent Fund for Vermont’s Children** , **Vermont’s Council of Developmental and Mental Health Services** and the **Vermont Technology Council**.

Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT & @Thunder_Truck
We hope to see you at our events next week, including the [AARP Vermont and VT Public TV Debate](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c0c87ebfea&e=19cebfe173). Stay up-to-date on our upcoming events [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8389571d27&e=19cebfe173).
***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_Responsible leadership with integrity_**
“I'm supporting Phil Scott because his word actually means something as he has the core integrity representative of genuine public servants and will make Vermont proud as Governor.” – Thomas Joseph, Wilmington (Source: Bennington Banner: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1e3456a1ea&e=19cebfe173))

**_Proven leadership_**
“I want a governor with proven leadership ability and someone who knows what it’s like to run a business in Vermont. And I want somebody who is willing to take on the status quo in government and restructure it so that it serves Vermonters again. That person is Phil Scott.” – Howard Manosh, Morrisville (Source: Times Argus: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0ea0049cd8&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil: We’ll leave no stone unturned to strengthen our economy_**
“To make real progress, and to truly strengthen our economy to generate revenue organically, rather than on the backs of tax payers, we need a comprehensive approach that comes at the problem from all angles. That’s why my plan for economic development covers topics from workforce training and taxes to healthcare, broadband and housing.” (Source: Burlington Free Press: [Full Article](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8f93edcb5f&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil: Vermonters can’t afford more taxes_**
"I think we've had enough of raising taxes and fees. I think we need to just take a deep breath, live within our means, tighten our belts and do whatever we can to not add to the burden on working families. It's across the board." (Source: WPTZ: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=afa6ce4875&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_Rep. Scheuermann: Where were they until now?_**
Rep. Heidi Scheuermann asks a very important question of Vermont’s Democrats in an op-ed in the _Stowe Reporter_ this week: Where were you on ethics reform? In a desperate attempt to gain traction, the Democrat party has worked hard to sling mud in Phil’s direction. But as Heidi points out, their aim is off! Rep. Scheuermann details her efforts to pass ethics reform since 2013, **only to be shot down repeatedly by the Democratic majority and Gov. Shumlin**. She writes, “So, **to now see Democrats waving the flag of ethics reform — after years of opposition — smacks of political opportunism**. After all, when they had the opportunity to speak up, and act, they refused.” Finally, she concludes “**If we want a governor who is the epitome of honesty and integrity in his public life, one who will demand it of others, one who will support state ethics reform that works, and, most importantly, one who will finally make the economy and affordability Montpelier’s highest priorities, there’s only one choice — Phil Scott.** ” Read her full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5a1c13dfac&e=19cebfe173) and read Phil’s plan to restore faith and trust in government [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ea030e106e&e=19cebfe173).

**_Democratic Activist Feeling the Need for Change_**
John Freitag, a self-described “Democratic activist,” writes an interesting piece about the problem with Party allegiance in a time of serious challenges and what he calls a distressing “shift toward centralized decision-making that ignores the strength and value of our local communities.” He cites Vermont Health Connect, Act 46 and energy legislation as examples of where that has happened in Vermont. Phil has always been a public servant who believes no individual or political party has a monopoly on good ideas. **As Governor, he will listen to any idea that will help improve our economy and make Vermont more affordable** , as he has done throughout his entire career. And, Phil has always done what is best for Vermont, regardless of whether that aligns with his party’s ideals or not. We hope you read [Mr. Freitag’s article](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=2c60fa14c0&e=19cebfe173), and know we welcome all Vermonters to our movement to make Vermont more affordable and move our economy forward!
*******
Thanks to everyone for continuing to follow and support our campaign. clean_body: | | | Phil Scott's Week In Action | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | Greetings, | We’re down to the final month of the general election, which means Team Scott is working extra hard to earn the vote of every Vermonter who wants to see the economy grow and Vermont become more affordable – which will be Phil’s top priorities as Governor. Check out this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign. EARLY VOTING IN PROGRESS – HAVE YOU VOTED FOR PHIL YET? Early voting is now open. You’ve kept us going throughout the campaign, now it’s time to pull the lever for Phil. Don’t wait until November 8. Voting early ensures nothing keeps you from the voting booth on election day, and helps us maximize our Get Out The Vote efforts for the remainder of the campaign. Click to find your town clerk's location or to request an absentee ballot. And, ICYMI, we’ve released our first TV ad of the General Election this week. The choice is clear! Watch it , share it with friends, and then get to your town clerk’s office to vote early! * PHIL’S ECONOMIC PLANS: WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT Last month Phil released a comprehensive economic development plan (which you can view ) that laid out his blueprint for economic growth. While his opponent is trying to jump on the economic messaging bandwagon in the final weeks of the campaign, Phil remains the only candidate with a comprehensive approach to strengthening the economy and making Vermont more affordable. This week, we want to share more about Phil’s proposals to grow our workforce and address our demographic challenges: Vermont faces significant demographic challenges as our population gets older, workers retire, and younger generations leave the state. A smaller workforce means a higher tax burden on those who remain. As Governor, Phil will support a number of actions to retain and attract more working-age Vermonters, grow our workforce and ease the tax burden. Those actions include supporting innovation in the public education system to make Vermont more appealing for young families; setting goals and working with Vermont colleges and universities to retain more college graduates ; investing in skilled workers and creating training programs to increase the number of skilled workers; and eliminating the “benefits cliff” to increase independence and bring more Vermonters into the workforce. Read more . Photo Credit: @PhilScottVT VERMONTERS DESERVE MORE FROM SHUMLIN ON HIS ALL-PAYER PLANS In his final months in office, Gov. Shumlin is working to push through a transition to an all-payer model, which would significantly impact our healthcare system, asking doctors to sign up as part of one giant Accountable Care Organization. This is a complicated transition, and one where the outcome is unknown. This week, Phil called on Gov. Shumlin and the Green Mountain Cardboard to add additional public presentations and more transparency on the model. “Vermonters deserve to have a detailed discussion about this before we move forward. We simply cannot afford any more expensive and unsuccessful healthcare experiments. I think it’s premature for the Shumlin Administration to lock Vermonters into an agreement, without first explaining how, exactly, it is going to work and what, if any, savings Vermonters can expect to see in the cost of care and in their insurance premiums,” Phil said in a statement. An editorial in the rightly points out that, yet again, Vermont will be charging forward on an experiment without knowing the impact on consumers and businesses. We saw how that worked with Vermont Health Connect! As the Messenger writes, “When the agreement is signed, Vermont will be the first in the nation to test the idea. No one knows whether it will be a good thing, or a disaster. But it would seem axiomatic that when we decide to turn upside down an industry that constitutes 20 percent of our economy that we do a more thorough job of analyzing the pros and cons. Publicly.” We agree. Read Phil’s full statement . INCREASING COSTS ON TRANSPORTATION WRONG FOR VERMONT As Phil said this week, his opponent’s so-called “cap and trade” plan for transportation would add unnecessary costs onto Vermont drivers and businesses, slow the economy and deepen the crisis of affordability facing many families and employers. In his response, Phil reinforced his commitment to growing the economy and making Vermont affordable “so we can afford to invest in priorities like early education and job training.” “I’m the only candidate in this race who knows that we need to be working to reduce the tax burden. To do that, we have to keep the state budget from growing faster than the economy and wages; stop imposing new taxes and fees; modernize state government; and make growing the economy the highest priority. If we don’t come to grips with this reality, we’ll continue to face higher taxes, a shrinking labor force and fewer resources to invest in programs we all support like early education, job training and substance abuse prevention and recovery,” Phil said. Read the full statement . Photo Credit: @ThunderTruck Forums and Friends Another exciting week of debates, events and hearing from hundreds of Vermonters about what matters to them – the economy! This week Phil and Team Scott joined Republicans from around the state at the Vermont Republican Party Fall Dinner , and stopped by Jet Service Envelope Company and the Vermont Pie & Pasta Company to check out some great small businesses. Phil also participated in the Vermont PBS debate and forums with American Academy of Pediatrics Vermont Chapter , Permanent Fund for Vermont’s Children , Vermont’s Council of Developmental and Mental Health Services and the Vermont Technology Council. Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT & @ThunderTruck We hope to see you at our events next week, including the . Stay up-to-date on our upcoming events . ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS Responsible leadership with integrity “I'm supporting Phil Scott because his word actually means something as he has the core integrity representative of genuine public servants and will make Vermont proud as Governor.” – Thomas Joseph, Wilmington (Source: Bennington Banner: ) Proven leadership “I want a governor with proven leadership ability and someone who knows what it’s like to run a business in Vermont. And I want somebody who is willing to take on the status quo in government and restructure it so that it serves Vermonters again. That person is Phil Scott.” – Howard Manosh, Morrisville (Source: Times Argus: ) Phil: We’ll leave no stone unturned to strengthen our economy “To make real progress, and to truly strengthen our economy to generate revenue organically, rather than on the backs of tax payers, we need a comprehensive approach that comes at the problem from all angles. That’s why my plan for economic development covers topics from workforce training and taxes to healthcare, broadband and housing.” (Source: Burlington Free Press: ) Phil: Vermonters can’t afford more taxes "I think we've had enough of raising taxes and fees. I think we need to just take a deep breath, live within our means, tighten our belts and do whatever we can to not add to the burden on working families. It's across the board." (Source: WPTZ: ) ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS Rep. Scheuermann: Where were they until now? Rep. Heidi Scheuermann asks a very important question of Vermont’s Democrats in an op-ed in the Stowe Reporter this week: Where were you on ethics reform? In a desperate attempt to gain traction, the Democrat party has worked hard to sling mud in Phil’s direction. But as Heidi points out, their aim is off! Rep. Scheuermann details her efforts to pass ethics reform since 2013, only to be shot down repeatedly by the Democratic majority and Gov. Shumlin. She writes, “So, to now see Democrats waving the flag of ethics reform — after years of opposition — smacks of political opportunism. After all, when they had the opportunity to speak up, and act, they refused.” Finally, she concludes “If we want a governor who is the epitome of honesty and integrity in his public life, one who will demand it of others, one who will support state ethics reform that works, and, most importantly, one who will finally make the economy and affordability Montpelier’s highest priorities, there’s only one choice — Phil Scott. ” Read her full article and read Phil’s plan to restore faith and trust in government . Democratic Activist Feeling the Need for Change John Freitag, a self-described “Democratic activist,” writes an interesting piece about the problem with Party allegiance in a time of serious challenges and what he calls a distressing “shift toward centralized decision-making that ignores the strength and value of our local communities.” He cites Vermont Health Connect, Act 46 and energy legislation as examples of where that has happened in Vermont. Phil has always been a public servant who believes no individual or political party has a monopoly on good ideas. As Governor, he will listen to any idea that will help improve our economy and make Vermont more affordable , as he has done throughout his entire career. And, Phil has always done what is best for Vermont, regardless of whether that aligns with his party’s ideals or not. We hope you read , and know we welcome all Vermonters to our movement to make Vermont more affordable and move our economy forward! Thanks to everyone for continuing to follow and support our campaign. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities every day – and share your thoughts – and . body:
| Team --

I was so excited to see that you’ve signed up to call voters from home as part of the Hillary for America call team! Passionate supporters like you are how we’re going to reach as many voters as possible and put Hillary over the top in November.

With votes already being cast around the country and with voter registration deadlines looming, it’s time to make sure everyone has what they need to cast their ballot this fall. We’re depending on our core supporters like you to ensure that happens. **[Start making calls to key people right now to make sure we get folks registered while there's still time. Can you make three calls today?](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjY3MzE3MzAS1&r=MjkzNzI1OTc4OTg1S0&b=0&j=OTAxNTA0OTM5S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

We've done the math: We know exactly in which areas we need every possible voter to turn out for Hillary, and in order for that to happen, we need to make sure people who live there are registered to vote.

These kinds of one-on-one interactions are our very best way of securing support for Hillary. But now, with these registration deadlines upon us, it is crucial that people in the areas we’ve identified hear directly from one of Hillary’s best supporters like you. **Just three calls will make a difference, and you can get started right now:**
[](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjY3MzE3MzAS1&r=MjkzNzI1OTc4OTg1S0&b=0&j=OTAxNTA0OTM5S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks so much -- you're the best!

Ashley
